original_abstract: Many sciences have made significant breakthroughs by adopting online tools that help organize, structure and mine information that is too detailed to be printed in journals. In this paper, we introduce OpenML, a place for machine learning researchers to share and organize data in fine detail, so that they can work more effectively, be more visible, and collaborate with others to tackle harder problems. We discuss how OpenML relates to other examples of networked science and what benefits it brings for machine learning research, individual scientists, as well as students and practitioners.
humanized_abstract: OpenML: Open Machine Learning As many fields of science have learned, using online tools to collect, order and relate data can make a huge difference when it comes to advancing the field. We describe OpenML, a new community that facilitates the sharing and (most importantly) the detailed description of datasets used in machine learning, and in that way makes research and communication more effective, visible and collaborative. We clarify the relation of OpenML to other forms of networked science and highlight the opportunities it presents for ML researchers, scientists, students and practitioners.

We clarify the relation of OpenML to other forms of networked science and highlight the opportunities it presents for ML researchers, scientists, students and practitioners.
